name: Trout in Lemon Butter
tagline: Whole trout pan-fried with lemon and brown butter
highlight: Brown butter, lemon, trout — French simplicity, no shellfish

hero.alt: Whole trout pan-fried with brown butter and lemon
hero.caption: trout — brown butter, lemon

healthFacts: Trout is omega-3-rich — comparable to salmon, with milder flavor.
Brown butter (beurre noisette) develops nutty notes from milk solids — chemistry, not nuts.

steps.title: Season and dredge
steps.detail: Pat trout dry inside and out. Salt, pepper, dredge lightly in flour, shake excess.
steps.tip: Flour absorbs surface moisture for clean crust.

steps.title: Pan-fry crisp
steps.detail: Heat olive oil + 2 tbsp butter in a wide skillet over medium-high. Lay trout in. Cook 4 min per side until skin is crisp and meat flakes.
steps.tip: Lay fish away from you so any oil splashes don't hit you.

steps.title: Brown the butter
steps.detail: Transfer trout to a plate. Wipe pan briefly. Add remaining 3 tbsp butter; melt and watch it foam, then turn light brown — about 2 min. Pull at the first nutty smell.
steps.tip: Brown butter goes from gold to burnt fast. Eyes on it.

steps.title: Lemon and pour
steps.detail: Off heat, squeeze lemon into the butter. Pour over trout. Scatter parsley.
steps.tip: Lemon hits hot butter and foams — that's the sound of dinner.
